perturbation.original_text: HDI shall have the Site fully operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet no later than sixty (60) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties. In the event that HDI fails to have the Site fully operational within seventy (70) days from the date of this Agreement, the Company shall have the right to terminate this Agreement without penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: HDI shall have the Site fully operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet no later than sixty (60) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties. In the event that HDI fails to have the Site fully operational within seventy (70) days from the date of this Agreement, the Company shall have the right to renegotiate the terms of the agreement instead of termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states the Company's right to terminate the agreement without penalty if HDI fails to have the site operational within 70 days. The modified version replaces this right of termination with a right to 'renegotiate the terms.' This creates a contradiction because it's now unclear if the Company can actually terminate without penalty. The introduction of 'renegotiate' creates an enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term; Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of that date (the "Effective Date") the Site becomes fully operational as set forth in writing and executed by both Parties and shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for an additional one year period on each anniversary of the Effective Date, unless terminated by either Party hereto upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other. Such notice shall specify the date on which this Agreement is to be terminated (the "Termination Date").
perturbation.justification: The original text provides the Company with a clear right to terminate the agreement without penalty if HDI fails to have the site operational within 70 days. The changed text replaces this with a right to 'renegotiate the terms.' The contradicted text states that the agreement can be terminated by either party upon 90 days written notice. The altered language in the changed text creates a contradiction because it is now unclear whether the company can terminate the agreement without penalty if the site is not fully operational within 70 days (as the changed text introduces a renegotiation clause) which conflicts with the contradicted text (which allows either party to terminate with 90 days written notice for any reason). This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because the placement of 'renegotiate' clause now conflicts with the contradicted clause regarding termination, creating uncertainty about the rights and obligations of each party.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall pay HDI thirty percent (3016) of the Net Sales in excess of Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Products on the Site including shipping and handling charges, minus refunds and exchanges.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall pay HDI thirty percent (30%) of the Net Sales in excess of Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month, subject to adjustments based on customer satisfaction scores. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Products on the Site including shipping and handling charges, minus refunds, exchanges, and potential discounts based on customer complaints.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a clear definition of 'Net Sales', including what is deducted from the revenue. The changed text introduces 'adjustments based on customer satisfaction scores' and 'potential discounts based on customer complaints' which are subjective and not clearly defined. This contradicts the initial clarity of the definition and creates uncertainty in how Net Sales are calculated, thus impacting payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall pay HDI thirty percent (30%) of the Net Sales in excess of Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month, subject to adjustments based on customer satisfaction scores. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Products on the Site including shipping and handling charges, minus refunds, exchanges, and potential discounts based on customer complaints.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'adjustments based on customer satisfaction scores' and 'potential discounts based on customer complaints' to the definition of 'Net Sales'. This introduces subjectivity and uncertainty, directly conflicting with the original clarity and creating a contradiction in how Net Sales are calculated and therefore how payments are determined, because the contradicted text relies on the new, ambiguous definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall have exclusive artistic and editorial control over the Site, including, without limitation, the implementation of the Content on the Site and the design and look and feel of the Site. Neither the Site nor any portion of thereof shall be deemed accepted and approved by the Company unless and until the Company accepts and approves same in writing to HDI. No portion of the Site shall be made available on the Internet without the consent of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall have artistic and editorial input over the Site, including, without limitation, the implementation of the Content on the Site and the design and look and feel of the Site, but HDI has final say. Neither the Site nor any portion of thereof shall be deemed accepted and approved by HDI. No portion of the Site shall be made available on the Internet once approved by HDI.
perturbation.explanation: The original text gives the Company 'exclusive' artistic and editorial control. The changed text states the Company has only 'input' and that HDI has 'final say'. This creates a direct contradiction, removing the exclusive control previously granted to the Company. Also, it contradicts the original approval process which requires the Company's approval. This change creates uncertainty in where the final decision lays regarding the site's content and design.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall have artistic and editorial input over the Site, including, without limitation, the implementation of the Content on the Site and the design and look and feel of the Site, but HDI has final say. Neither the Site nor any portion of thereof shall be deemed accepted and approved by HDI. No portion of the Site shall be made available on the Internet once approved by HDI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement by altering the 'exclusive artistic and editorial control' of the Company to mere 'input' while granting HDI 'final say'. This shift clashes with the initial allocation of exclusive control, creating an in-text contradiction as per the Structural Flaws definition. Also, the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, and by making this change, it contradicts what was originally stated.
